name: Loudoun County Public Schools
districtFact: Loudoun County Public Schools formally observes Diwali, Lunar New Year, Eid al-Fitr, and Yom Kippur as district-wide student holidays — one of the few large US school districts to include all four on the official calendar.

about.title: About Loudoun County Public Schools
about.content:
Loudoun County Public Schools covers the full county — from Ashburn and Sterling in the east, through Leesburg (the county seat), to Purcellville and the smaller towns along the Blue Ridge foothills in the west. The eastern corridor, built around Data Center Alley and the Silver Line extension, is dense suburbia with subdivision developments packed against each other along Route 7 and Route 28. The western third is farmland, equestrian estates, and wineries — a rural character that hasn't changed much even as the rest of the county grew.

Most enrollment growth comes from the eastern side, where the district has opened new elementary schools nearly every year since the early 2010s to keep pace with residential construction in Ashburn, Brambleton, South Riding, and One Loudoun. That pace means attendance zone boundaries shift frequently. A school listed in a builder's marketing materials or an MLS listing may not be the assigned school by the time a family moves in. Families moving during the summer usually verify their assignment through the LCPS boundary lookup at lcps.org before closing on a home — neighborhood names, zip codes, and what a selling agent says don't always reflect current boundaries.

about.title: What to Know Before the First School Year
about.content: LCPS formally observes Diwali, Lunar New Year, Eid al-Fitr, and Yom Kippur as student holidays — school is closed on these days for everyone, not just families who observe them. Each date falls on a different day each year because all four follow lunar or lunisolar calendars that don't land on the same Gregorian date from year to year. Because the dates change every year, it's worth checking the current LCPS calendar rather than using last year's schedule. LCPS also starts the school year in mid-August, typically about a week before Fairfax County and Prince William County — a gap that matters for households coordinating childcare or activities across multiple NoVA districts.

content: Diwali, Lunar New Year, Eid al-Fitr, and Yom Kippur are student holidays in LCPS — school is closed for everyone, not just families who observe them. Each date changes year to year with the religious calendar; last year's LCPS calendar isn't reliable for this year's dates.

LCPS starts about one week before Fairfax County and Prince William County — a difference that shows up in August, when neighboring district families haven't started school yet. Households with children in multiple NoVA districts, or parents whose employer runs on a neighboring district's calendar, deal with that offset directly.

In eastern Loudoun — Ashburn, Brambleton, South Riding, One Loudoun — school boundaries shift when new campuses open. The boundary lookup at lcps.org reflects current zone assignments; builder and agent materials in active construction areas frequently don't.

districtFaqs.q: Does LCPS observe Diwali and other cultural holidays as school holidays?
districtFaqs.a: Yes. School is closed district-wide for Diwali, Lunar New Year, Eid al-Fitr, and Yom Kippur — full student holidays, not optional observances. The specific dates shift year to year; check the current LCPS calendar at lcps.org.

districtFaqs.q: When does Loudoun County start school?
districtFaqs.a: LCPS typically begins in mid-August. In 2026–2027, the first day is August 17 — about a week before Fairfax County and Prince William County, which start around August 24. The earlier start means LCPS also ends the school year on a slightly different schedule. For current-year dates, check the official calendar at lcps.org.

districtFaqs.q: How does LCPS compare to Fairfax County Public Schools?
districtFaqs.a: LCPS and Fairfax County Public Schools are adjacent districts serving different parts of Northern Virginia. The two districts have similar school year structures, but LCPS starts about a week earlier than Fairfax and formally observes Diwali, Lunar New Year, Eid al-Fitr, and Yom Kippur as student holidays — days that Fairfax doesn't include on its calendar. Spring Break dates have aligned between the two districts in recent years, but aren't guaranteed to match every year.

districtFaqs.q: How often do school boundaries change in Loudoun County?
districtFaqs.a: Boundary changes in eastern Loudoun are regular, not occasional. The district has opened new elementary schools nearly every year in Ashburn, Brambleton, and surrounding communities, and each opening redraws the surrounding attendance zones. Homebuyers in recently built subdivisions have found their assigned school changed between the time they purchased a home and the time they enrolled. The current zone assignment is at lcps.org; real estate listings and builder materials don't always reflect the latest boundaries.

districtFaqs.q: How common are snow days in Loudoun County?
districtFaqs.a: Loudoun County gets real winter weather. Closures and two-hour delays happen most years, concentrated in January and February — some years bring one or two events, others run through several. LCPS builds buffer days into the calendar, but extended snow events can require makeup days. Closures are announced through the LCPS website, the ParentSquare notification system, and local television and radio.

livingHere.intro: Eastern and western Loudoun feel like different counties. Ashburn and the Route 28 corridor are built around data centers, metro access, and subdivisions that keep expanding outward. The western third is farmland, wineries, and small towns — families who choose that side accept longer commutes to Dulles and D.C. in exchange for the rural character.

highlights.label: Cultural Holidays on the School Calendar
highlights.detail: Diwali, Lunar New Year, Eid al-Fitr, and Yom Kippur are official student holidays on the LCPS calendar — not optional observances, but no-school days for everyone. The eastern part of Loudoun has large South Asian, East Asian, Muslim, and Jewish communities, concentrated in the tech-sector corridors of eastern Loudoun. For parents who don't observe these holidays, the practical effect is the same: school is closed and childcare or alternate plans are needed. The dates shift year to year because Diwali, Eid, and Lunar New Year all follow lunar or lunisolar calendars — last year's dates don't apply to this year.

highlights.label: School Zone Boundaries in Eastern Loudoun
highlights.detail: The fastest-growing parts of Loudoun County — Ashburn, Brambleton, South Riding, One Loudoun — are also where school zone boundaries shift most often. When the district opens a new elementary school in the eastern corridor, the surrounding attendance zones redraw. Households in nearby neighborhoods may find they're assigned to the new campus rather than the one they expected. This has happened repeatedly over the past decade. Anyone buying or renting in eastern Loudoun should check their specific school assignment at lcps.org before signing, not after.

highlights.label: Data Center Corridor and Corporate Relocations
highlights.detail:
The Route 7/Route 28 corridor hosts more data center capacity than any other geography on Earth — AWS, Microsoft, Google, and dozens of co-location providers operate facilities there, and much of that workforce lives in LCPS attendance zones in Ashburn, Sterling, and South Riding. Mid-year enrollment driven by corporate relocations is routine at eastern Loudoun campuses.

For families being relocated to Loudoun by a tech employer, timing matters. Many corporate move dates follow project starts or lease cycles, not the academic calendar. Initiating contact with LCPS enrollment before the physical move — even a few weeks ahead — typically shortens the gap between arriving in Northern Virginia and the student's first school day. Required documentation is on the LCPS enrollment page at lcps.org.

At the high school level, credit evaluation for mid-year transfers from other states is handled at the campus level; contacting the receiving school counselor before the move is more efficient than waiting until arrival.

highlights.label: Winter Weather
highlights.detail: Loudoun County gets real winter weather. Snowstorms and ice events in January and February are frequent enough that most years include at least one closure and several two-hour delays. LCPS builds buffer days into the calendar for this reason, but extended snow events can push makeup days into late spring. Residents relocating from the Deep South or from warmer climates should expect occasional full-day closures and adjusted schedules during winter months.

relatedDistricts.name: Fairfax County Public Schools
relatedDistricts.reason: FCPS is the adjacent district to the east and the most common comparison point for Loudoun families; LCPS starts about a week before Fairfax, and the two districts' cultural holiday observances differ — Fairfax doesn't formally observe Diwali and Lunar New Year as student holidays.

relatedDistricts.name: Prince William County Public Schools
relatedDistricts.reason: Prince William County is the adjacent district to the south, with a similar school year structure to Fairfax; households in the southern Loudoun or northern Prince William borderlands should confirm which district their specific address falls in.

relatedDistricts.name: Montgomery County Public Schools
relatedDistricts.reason: Montgomery County, Maryland serves the DC suburbs directly across the Potomac from Loudoun; families choosing between Northern Virginia and the Maryland suburbs often compare the two districts, which serve similar tech-sector workforces and have similar school year structures.
